treatsAilments.notes: Reduces LDL oxidation and plaque formation
Oleuropein has antiviral and hepatoprotective properties

herbs.safetyNotes: Generally safe. May cause die-off reactions when fighting infections.

treatsAilments.notes: Exceptionally high in iron, vitamin C, and protein
Rich in vitamins, minerals, and amino acids
Isothiocyanates may help lower blood sugar
Rich in zinc, iron, and protein for nail health
Highly nutritious; helps address nutrient deficiencies in celiac

herbs.safetyNotes: Leaves are very safe. Avoid bark and root during pregnancy (may cause uterine contractions).

herbs.name: Arnica

treatsAilments.notes: Topical anti-inflammatory; reduces bruising and swelling
Topical use comparable to ibuprofen gel for hand OA
Only for unbroken skin; reduces bruising
Topical pain relief for wrist area
Reduces post-surgical bruising, swelling, and pain

herbs.safetyNotes: TOPICAL USE ONLY on unbroken skin. Internal use of herbal arnica is TOXIC.

treatsAilments.notes: Approved in Germany for cough and bronchitis; antispasmodic

Antimicrobial and soothing for vocal cords

herbs.safetyNotes: Very safe as culinary herb and tea. Essential oil should be diluted.

treatsAilments.notes: Immunomodulatory triterpenes may help balance immune response
Immunomodulatory; may help regulate overactive immune response

Traditional mushroom of immortality; potent antioxidant

Enhances oxygen uptake and altitude adaptation

Alcohol-free preparations can soothe dry skin

herbs.safetyNotes: Topical use is very safe. Internal use not recommended in large amounts.

May reduce hot flashes and improve skin
Essential fatty acids support hair health

GLA supports hormonal balance and reduces inflammation
